# Spec context: §11.0 — Introduction to directives

## Source

- Upstream: <https://abcnotation.com/wiki/abc:standard:v2.1#introduction_to_directives>
- Cached at: `crates/kaijutsu-abc/docs/abc-spec-cache.md` (line 2414)
- Section: 11. Stylesheet directives and pseudo-comments
- Subsection: 11.0 Introduction to directives

Reproduced verbatim from the ABC v2.1 standard by Chris Walshaw,
under CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 — see `tests/fixtures/spec/README.md`.

## Preceding prose

> In particular, it is intended that abc 2.3 will address markup and embedding and at that point a number of the text-based directives, together with other widely accepted forms, will be formally incorporated.
> For the moment, section 11 is retained mostly unchanged from abc 2.0 (save for typo corrections) but, as a result of the foregoing, the whole of section 11 and all stylesheet directives should regarded as *VOLATILE*.
> A **stylesheet directive** is a line that starts with `%%`, followed by a directive that gives instructions to typesetting or player programs.
> *Examples:*
> Alternatively, any [stylesheet directive](#stylesheet_directive_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") may be written as an `I:instruction` field although this is not recommended for usages which have not been standardised (i.e. it is not recommended for any directives described in section 11).
> *Examples:* Not recommended.
